You are verifier #1, independent of all other verifiers. Do NOT confirm findings optimistically — only confirm if you can independently verify the issue exists in the actual code at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay.

Finding F-027: "bootstrap_tracing() called after OtelTracer construction — global provider may not be set when first span is emitted"
File: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py (line 342)
Description: bootstrap_tracing() called at line 342 AFTER tracer = tracer or OtelTracer() at line 274. If custom tracer emits spans in constructor before bootstrap_tracing() returns, those spans go to pre-registration no-op provider.
PoC: Pass custom tracer that emits span in constructor. That span goes to the pre-registration no-op provider.

Instructions:
1. Read the actual file at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
2. Look at the code at line 342 and surrounding context
3. Independently verify whether the described problem EXISTS in the current code
4. Check if the PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written
5. Consider whether this is already fixed (pass-1 #101-#122, pass-2 #124-#156 fixed many issues)
6. Return your independent verdict

Confirmed = true ONLY if: (a) the code problem exists as described, (b) the PoC is reproducible from current code, (c) it is a genuine production-readiness concern. Set confirmed=false for speculative issues, issues already fixed, or issues where the PoC does not match what the code actually does.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: The finding claims `bootstrap_tracing()` is called at line 342 AFTER `tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()` at line 274. I can see both are present. Now I need to check whether `OtelTracer.__init__` emits any spans in its constructor.

content.content:
1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
75	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
77	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
78	            )
79	            _provider = provider
80	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
81	            _instrument_langchain()
82	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
83	            return _provider
84	        except Exception:
85	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced.
86	            return None
87	
88	
89	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
90	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
91	    try:
92	        from opentelemetry import trace
93	
94	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
95	            _log.warning(
96	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
97	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
98	            )
99	    except Exception:
100	        pass
101	
102	
103	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
104	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
105	    try:
106	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
107	
108	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
109	    except Exception:
110	        pass
111	
112	
113	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
114	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
115	
116	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
117	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
118	    """
119	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
120	    if not callable(flush):
121	        return
122	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
123	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
124	        try:
125	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
126	
127	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
128	                _safe(flush)
129	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
130	                if callable(prior):
131	                    prior(signum, frame)
132	
133	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
134	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
135	            pass
136	
137	
138	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
139	    try:
140	        fn()
141	    except Exception:
142	        pass
143	
144	
145	@contextmanager
146	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
147	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
148	
149	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
150	    """
151	    if run_id is None:
152	        yield
153	        return
154	    try:
155	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
156	    except ImportError:
157	        yield
158	        return
159	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
160	    try:
161	        yield
162	    finally:
163	        context.detach(token)
164	
165	
166	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
167	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
168	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
169	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
170	
171	
172	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
173	class Tracer(Protocol):
174	    @contextmanager
175	    def span(
176	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
177	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
178	
179	
180	class OtelTracer:
181	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
182	
183	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
184	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
185	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
186	    """
187	
188	    @contextmanager
189	    def span(
190	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
191	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
192	        try:
193	            from opentelemetry import trace
194	        except ImportError:
195	            yield
196	            return
197	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
198	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
199	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
200	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
201	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
202	            yield
203	
204	
205	class UnconfiguredTracer:
206	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
207	
208	    @contextmanager
209	    def span(
210	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
211	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
212	        raise NotImplementedError(
213	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
214	            "test double"
215	        )
216	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
217

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me analyze the finding:

1. `OtelTracer.__init__` does NOT exist — the class has no `__init__` method at all. Its only method is `span()`, which is a context manager.
2. `OtelTracer()` construction at line 274 simply creates a plain Python object with no `__init__` logic, so no spans are emitted during construction.
3. The `span()` method is called only at line 346 (`with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):`), which is well after `bootstrap_tracing()` is called at line 342.
4. The PoC premise — "Pass custom tracer that emits span in constructor" — would require a *custom* tracer (not `OtelTracer`) with a span-emitting `__init__`. But that's user-injected code; the pipeline itself doesn't create the custom tracer — it's passed in as the `tracer` parameter. If a caller passes in a custom tracer that emits spans in its constructor, those spans are emitted before `execute_run()` is even called (at the call site), not inside the function at line 274 (which would only assign `tracer or OtelTracer()` — and if `tracer` is already provided, `OtelTracer()` is never called at all).
5. There is no ordering bug with the default `OtelTracer` — it has no constructor side effects, and `span()` is only used after `bootstrap_tracing()` is called.

236	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
237	
238	    return _log
239	
240	
241	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
242	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
243	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
244	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
245	
246	
247	def run_study(
248	    plan: StudyPlan,
249	    *,
250	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
251	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
252	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,

254	    clock: Clock | None = None,

256	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
257	) -> StudyResult:
258	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
259	
260	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
261	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
262	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
263	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
264	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
265	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
266	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
267	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
268	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
269	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
270	
271	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
272	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
273	    """
274	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()

content.text: This confirms my analysis. The function signature accepts `tracer: Tracer | None = None` and only constructs `OtelTracer()` as a fallback. `OtelTracer` has no `__init__` and emits no spans during construction. The first actual span usage is at line 346, after `bootstrap_tracing()` at line 342.

input.reasoning:
The finding's core premise does not hold for the actual code. OtelTracer (defined in /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py) has no __init__ method whatsoever — it is a plain class with only a span() context manager. Constructing OtelTracer() at line 274 emits zero spans; all span emission happens via the span() context manager, which is first called at line 346 (with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):), which is after bootstrap_tracing() at line 342.

The PoC scenario — "pass custom tracer that emits span in constructor" — requires user-injected code. If a caller passes a custom tracer with a span-emitting constructor, those constructor spans fire before run_study() is called at all (at the call site), not at line 274, because line 274 only executes OtelTracer() when tracer is None. If a non-None custom tracer is provided, its constructor has already run before the function body executes.

There is no production-readiness bug here: the default OtelTracer path is safe (no constructor spans), the first span from the pipeline itself is emitted after bootstrap_tracing() completes, and the ordering described in the finding is not a realistic issue with the actual OtelTracer implementation. The issue is a false positive.
